1 COUNTY POLICE CAR LOST WHEN FLOOD WATERS COVER ROAD.

LODGEPOOL CREEK OVERFLOWED ITS BANKS ROUGHLY 3.3 MILES WEST OF TOWN AND FLOWED EAST BETWEEN US HWY 385 AND THE RAILROAD. RAILROAD AND HWY NARROWED NEAR THE COOP IN LODGEPOLE CAUSING WATERS TO BUILD UP PRESSURE AND THEN ERODE THE RAILROAD CAUSING FLOODING IN SOUTHERN PARTS OF LODGEPOLE. FIFTY HOMES WERE EVACUATED IN THE SOUTH PART OF TOWN BY 11 PM. DURING THE WORST OF THE FLOODING WATER WAS 5 FT. DEEP IN PLACES...WITH OTHER AREAS 2-3 BLOCKS WIDE AND 5 BLOCKS LONG HAVING WATER 2-3 FT. DEEP.
GROUND WAS COMPLETELY COVERED WITH HAIL UP TO QUARTER SIZE.

SPOTTER STATED THAT IN AN AREA FROM ARDMORE SOUTH DAKOTA TO SOUTH OF THE NEBRASKA STATE LINE HOUSE WINDOWS BROKEN BY HAIL. ESTIMATED 60 MPH WINDS. CAR WINDOWS BROKEN. OVER AN INCH OF RAIN. GRAVEL PILES ALONG HWY 71 BLOWN ACROSS ROAD. TIME OF OCCURRANCE ESTIMATED.

HAIL BROKE WINDOWS AND STRIPPED PAINT ALONG THE NORTH SIDE OF THE HOUSE. THE BROKEN WINDOWS ALLOWED RAIN TO ENTER THE HOUSE. ALL THE TREES WERE STRIPPED OF LEAVES. THE SPOTTER SAID IT LOOKED LIKE A BOMB WENT OFF.

HAIL DAMAGE TO MANY CARS, SHATTERED WINDOWS AT WALMART PARKING LOT. MANY BUSINESS SIGNS BLOWN OUT OR WITH HOLES FROM HAIL. MANY STREET AND SEVERALY POWER LINES DOWN. THREE TRAILER HOMES SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. ONE WAS FLIPPED ON ITS ROOF AND TH OTHER TWO WERE BLOWN TO PIECES INTO A NEARBY FIELD. ORANGE CONSTRUCTION BARRRES TOSSED 3/4 OF A MILE FROM THE HIGHWAY. FLASH FLOODING ALONG MANY STREETS WITH MAPLE HAVING A FOOT OF WATER FLOWING DOWN IT. THE HAIL WAS 1.75 INCHES.
